observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) instruct the MA to (i) comply with the regulation prescribed by MNGAS in recording checks issued in the accounting books; (ii) immediately effect the adjustment of cancelled checks and accounting errors in the books to reflect the accurate balance of the CIB-LCCA account; and (iii) verify the correctness of RCDs and RAAFs; and (b) MT to continuously comply with the daily and intact deposit of collections.
observations.managementAction: The MA recorded checks based on the Report of Checks Issued prepared by the MTO, as prescribed. The MA continued to verify the correctness of RCDs and RAAFs. MT continued to comply with the daily and intact deposit of collections.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II – Observation No. 1 in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the MA to (i) record all PPEs purchased or constructed by the Municipality as prescribed under IPSAS 14 and other COA guidelines pertinent to the PPE account; (ii) designate personnel to diligently track down the cost, acquisition date, and other details of items of PPE recorded in the GL to improve the lapsing schedule, and accurately compute the annual depreciation expense; and (b) instruct the MA and MT to continuously reconcile their property records and resolve any discrepancies in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. We reiterated and the LCE agreed to create a titling committee responsible in gathering all ownership documents other than Transfer Certificate of Title (TCTs) to facilitate the titling of parcels of land purportedly owned by the Municipality, and provide a budget for the cost of titling.
observations.managementAction: The MA recorded PPEs and monitored additions/disposals. The MA and MT performed periodic reconciliations and monitored changes in PPE balances due to purchase and disposal.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II – Observation No. 2 in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the ME to prepare and submit to the MA a copy of the Certificates of Inspection and Completion for the completed projects; and (b) (i) the MA to reclassify the completed projects from the CIP account to their respective PPE accounts in accordance with Section 50 of the MNGAS-LGU Volume I, and (ii) recognize under the CIP account the cost of ongoing projects funded by TF.
observations.managementAction: The ME prepared project completion documents and submitted to the MA for reclassification. The MA reviewed submitted documents for reclassification and recognition.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II – Observation No. 2 in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to (a) make representations with identified source agencies such as the DOH and DSWD, among others, and inquire if the Municipality has any outstanding unliquidated fund transfers to facilitate appropriate action; and (b) make the necessary adjustments in the books to reclassify receipts erroneously lodged to the Due to LGUs account instead of the income account.
observations.managementAction: The MA reclassified the accounts lodged to the Due to LGUs account through unnumbered JEV dated April 2024. The MA made representations and coordinated with source agencies to clarify outstanding items under Due to NGAs.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II – Observation No. 3 in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) reorganize the SWMB to include the lacking members pursuant to Section 12 of the Act; (b) require the MENRO Chief to (i) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the review and passage of the Municipal Environmental Code; and (ii) facilitate the drafting and approval of the IRR for the Ecological Solid Waste Management Ordinance to establish clear guidelines in the implementation of its provisions; (c) revisit the 10-Year SWMP, formulate and implement strategies to catch-up the execution of the programmed PPAs and their objectives; (d) formulate and implement source reduction, waste avoidance, and waste diversion measures taking into account the aftermath of the pandemic and current socio-economic environment; (e) tap the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Budget Office to look for income sources to fund solid waste management PPAs other than waste disposal; and (f) include in the annual budget sufficient funds for the repair and maintenance of machines used for waste processing and salary of capable operators.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order No.09 Series of 2024 dated February 12, 2024 reorganized the SWMB to include the lacking members. The MENRO Chief exerted effort to coordinate the review and passage of the Municipal Environmental Code and prepared revisions of the 10-year SWMP. Some initiatives for source reduction and funding were identified, but full implementation and budgeting for MRF machinery repair and operators' salaries remain outstanding.
observations.reason: The MENRO facilitated the drafting and review; however, IRR for the Ecological Solid Waste Management Ordinance was not yet approved. Source reduction measures, additional funding mechanisms, and budgeting for machine repair and operators remain to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) the MT to (i) continue to comply with the provisions of RA No. 7160, particularly on the collection of business tax, of tax monitoring and chasing of erring businesses, and examination of the book of accounts; (ii) proactively coordinate with the SB for the review and passage of the proposed revised revenue code; and (iii) implement stricter internal control measures over review procedures before report submission; and (b) the MT and MA to reconcile their records to ensure accurately reported data.
observations.managementAction: The MT continued tax monitoring, chasing erring businesses and examining books of accounts. The MT proactively coordinated with the Sangguniang Bayan for review and passage of proposed ordinances. MT implemented stricter internal control measures. Reconciliation between MT and MA records was not yet completed.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of MT and MA records to ensure accurate reporting remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the MT to direct the DO to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the handling of CAs by maintaining and daily/regular recording of transactions in the cashbook, and timely liquidation of CAs; and (b) the MA to promptly update the ledgers, and review the monthly CA monitoring report to correct negative balances, if any.
observations.managementAction: The MT directed the DO to adhere to rules on handling CAs and maintain daily recording and timely liquidation. The MA was requested to update ledgers and review monthly CA monitoring reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA and Human Resources and Management Officer to (a) reconcile the employees’ GSIS records to ensure that monthly contributions and loan payments tally with the remitted amounts; and (b) investigate the noted discrepancies to prevent the recurrence of the same mistakes.
observations.managementAction: Efforts to reconcile employees' GSIS records and coordinate with HRMO to update records were undertaken but reconciliation and investigation remain ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA to (a) diligently review the monthly SAWT to ensure full remittance of withheld taxes to the BIR within the prescribed period; and (b) trace the details of the prior years’ unremitted taxes to facilitate prompt remittance to the BIR.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

To address the budgetary constraints:

Require the Municipal Health Office, Municipal Engineering Office, Municipal Budget Office, and other offices concerned, to identify the funding required after duly considering the necessary activities for the operation of COVID-19 facility;

Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide budget augmentations, if necessary, to fund the activities;

If there are funding gaps, these shall be submitted to Provincial Government of Davao del Sur for resolution in coordination with DOH, DILG and the DBM as provided in DOH-DILG JAO No. 2020-001.

To comply with the Infrastructure Requirements:

Instruct the Municipal Health Office to provide for the necessary specifications required in the COVID-19  facility infrastructures; 

 Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide for: (a) a construction plan duly considering the specifications submitted by the Municipal Health Office; (b) total estimated cost for the facility; (c) the submission of PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the procurement of materials 

Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the needed materials for the construction.

To comply with the Supplies and Other Resources Requirements:

Instruct the Municipal Health Office to: (a) identify the supplies and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities; and, (b) submit the PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the procurement of materials.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a)
Municipal Accountant (MA) to (i) restore back to the CIB-LCCA account the stale
and cancelled checks amounting to ₱42,942.09 and ₱2,349,332.64, respectively, to
reflect the true balance of the CIB-LCCA account; and (ii) observe the proper
accounting treatment on unclaimed checks at the end of the year in accordance with
Section 79 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1; and (b) MA and Municipal Treasurer
(MT) to conduct quarterly reconciliation of their cash records and recognize
immediately in the books any unrecorded transactions affecting the CIB-LCCA
account.

1.10. Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

2.8. We recommended that the LCE require the MT to (a) direct the COs to remit
the collections to the LO at the end of the day to ensure their safety; (b) prepare the
CRAAF in accordance with the rules and regulations for an accurate reporting of
issued ORs; (c) regularly monitor the CTC forms issued to barangays; and (d) require
the barangays concerned to turn-over the issued CTC that remained in their custody
for a long period of time.

2.9. Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

3.17. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Property Officer to
immediately conduct actual count of inventories and forward the Report of Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the MA for recording in the books; (b) MA to (i)
prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards to record the acquisition, issuance and
disposal of inventories; and (ii) to record all acquisitions of inventories to the
appropriate Inventory account and recognize the equivalent expense account upon
receipt of RSMI supported with RIS from the Property Unit; and (c) Property Officer
to (i) use the RIS in the issuance of inventories to end-users, and summarize the RIS
monthly through the RSMI; and (ii) submit a Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances supported with Acknowledgement Receipts of the
donations in-kind, Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not
necessarily in the required form, and Inventory of the remaining undistributed items,
if any.

3.18. Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

4.17. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and MT as General
Services Office (GSO) Chief to (i) maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards,
respectively, to substantiate the existence, completeness and reliability of items
composing the respective PPE accounts; and (ii) facilitate the reconciliation process
for identification of any discrepancies in PPE records; (b) MA to (i) prepare a lapsing
schedule of depreciation for PPE items to support the recognition of allowance for
depreciation and submit the same to the Audit Team for review/audit; (ii) make the
necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books the instructional materials
that were still recorded under the PPE account but already distributed to end-users;
and (iii) classify/recognize the small value items costing less than ₱10,000.00 under
the appropriate inventory/ expense account; and (c) MT to (i) coordinate with the
Municipal Assessor in obtaining copies of the TCTs of lands allegedly owned by the
Municipality to substantiate ownership; and (ii) coordinate with the MA in taking
immediate action to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of the unserviceable items.

4.18. Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) maintain PPE ledger cards to substantiate the existence, completeness and reliability of items composing the respective PPE accounts, and to facilitate the reconciliation process with the MTO for identification of any discrepancies in PPE records; 

(ii) set up a spreadsheet application or lapsing schedule for the PPE items to facilitate the computation of the periodic depreciation for each PPE item and submit the same to the Audit Team for review/audit; and 

(iii) make the necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books the instructional materials that were still recorded under the PPE account but already distributed to end-users; and 

(b) Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) keep the original copies of the OR/CR of motor vehicles and renew their registration to avoid additional expenses from payment of damages to third person liabilities and penalties in case of vehicular accidents; and 

(ii) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor Office in obtaining copies of the TCTs of lands allegedly owned by the Municipality to substantiate ownership.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) work for the immediate enactment of the Municipality’s appropriation ordinance by the Sangguniang Bayan to prevent the same deficiency in the future; 

(b) instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to fast track the implementation of other infrastructure projects in accordance with the AIP; 
(c) require the Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning Development Office to specifically identify the PPAs in the AIP of the 20% DF by sectoral type; and

(d) require the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to 

(i) ensure that the documentary requirements are submitted for the procurement of infrastructure projects with reference to RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and 

(ii) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking bidding documents and other supporting documents on progress and final payments to preclude the noted transactions from being suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council 

(i) efficiently and effectively plan the LDRRMF for PPAs that have an impact in mitigating risk at the strike of a disaster; and 

(ii) ensure that the PPAs to be charged against the LDRRMF are incorporated in the LDRRMP and integrated into the Municipal’s AIP pursuant to the guidelines set forth in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and 

(b) require the Municipal Accountant to submit on a monthly frequency the report on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF based on the required format of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE ensure the 

(a) effective implementation of solid waste management strategies such as the conduct of intensive information campaign and regular monitoring of the Ordinance on prohibition in the use of plastic and Styrofoam within the locality, and other related policies; and 

(b) timely implementation of projects that will further contribute to the objectives of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE 

(a) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the rationalization and revision of the Local Revenue Code and undertake General Revision of the Real Property Assessment (GRRPA) to generate more revenues for the local government; and 

(b) identify the problems that face real property tax collection of the Municipality and enforce the procedures and remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided by Section 174 of RA No. 7160 to improve revenue collection.

We recommended that the LCE require the Head of the HRMO 

(a) to continuously comply with the pertinent rules and regulations relating to the hiring, supervision and management of JOS, casuals, and consultants; and 

(b) ensure that the JOs sign the contract of service to signify their consent on the terms and conditions of the contract.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) submit written explanation on the discrepancy between the amounts posted in the GL and actual remittance of withheld taxes to BIR per Check Disbursement Journal CkDJ; and 

(b) henceforth, comply with the pertinent rules and regulations pertaining to the withholding of taxes for income payments of employees under job order contracts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to

(a) submit written explanation on the discrepancy between the amounts posted in the general ledger and actual remittance of withheld contributions; and 

(b) henceforth, closely monitor the said accounts by conducting monthly reconciliation between the recognized amounts and the actual remittances made.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to 

(a) include in the GPB the PPAs that are based on gender issues and recommendations in accordance with RA No. 9710; and 

(b) provide a monitoring and evaluation framework for assessing GAD results and outcomes that shall be the basis for strengthening the mainstreaming of GAD programs and activities in the next planning period.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

1. Stop the practice of depositing SEF and TF in Time Deposit since these funds are created with specific purposes; and

2. Take appropriate action for the unauthorized placement of special funds, the issuance of manager’s checks, and for non-compliance with the guidelines on disbursements, so that the internal control will remain operative and effective.

We recommended management to:

1. Prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical inventory including the time frame to complete the inventory count and the preparation and submission of the RPCPEE taking into consideration the present condition and location of the properties; and

2. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to coordinate and prepare/update property records to facilitate reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence and condition of the properties.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

1. Execute a MOA with the barangays setting forth as one of the conditions that the fund transfer is subject for liquidation pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and

2. irect the Municipal Accountant to record the fund transfer under Due from LGUs account instead of Subsidy to LGUs for proper recording, reporting and presentation in the financial statements.

To record local road networks in the books of accounts, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:  

GSO and the Municipal Engineer to conduct the physical inventory on the Local Road Network to come up with the Inventory Report on the Local Road Network; and

Municipal Accountant to initially record beginning balance of Local Road Networks in the books of accounts using as basis the Registry of Public Infrastructures and the Inventory Report on the Local Road Network.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to adhere to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by including in the POW and project design the cost of the public notification/information at the least possible cost or most economically effective means; and

Henceforth, billboard or signboard having vital information shall be installed at the project site to enhance public awareness and transparency.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Budget Officer properly monitor the budget allocation for the PPAs and help the Municipal Development Council (MDC) in the re-evaluation of unimplemented projects;

The MDC with the assistance of the MPDO, reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed development projects. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and

The Budget Officer and MDC appropriate sufficient funds for the complete implementation of the identified projects.

We recommended management to:

Consider redirecting priorities from regular programs and activities to other major concerns that need immediate action by the LGU especially the purchasing of lots for relocation sites of those homeless victims of calamity;  and

Maximize the utilization of LDRRMF by formulating programs/projects/activities particularly for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation so that the LGU’s ultimate objective of protecting the lives and properties of its constituents will be realized.

Recommendations:                            We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:                                  
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Special Account for Market and Cemetery operations, as well as the 20% Development fund; and

2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cash books for Market and Cemetery operations and 20% Development fund account.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1. Direct the GFPS to conduct annual updates on gender analysis, through attendance in trainings and seminars relative to GAD. Enjoin participation of all personnel to attend said trainings and seminars; and

2. Direct the GFPS to use the Municipality’s existing GAD database in conducting gender analysis so that real gender issues will be addressed and to ensure that GAD PPAs are gender-responsive.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Stop the practice of depositing SEF and TF in Time Deposit since these funds
are created with specific purposes; and
2. Take appropriate action for the unauthorized placement of special funds, the
issuance of manager’s checks, and for non-compliance with the guidelines on
disbursements, so that the internal control will remain operative and effective.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Direct the Municipal Health Officer to prepare and submit the utilization report
with complete supporting documents to the accounting office;
2. Enforce measures such as “No utilization report No requisition” policy on
supplies for all departments;
3. Require the Inventory Committee to regularly conduct physical count of
unused supplies as prescribed in Section 124 - Volume I of NGAS Manual and
submit report to the office of COA; and
4. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries on the
affected accounts using the inventory report.

Recommendations:
We recommended management to:
• Prepare a plan for the conduct of a complete physical inventory including the
time frame to complete the inventory count and the preparation and
submission of the RPCPEE taking into consideration the present condition and
location of the properties; and
• Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to coordinate and prepare/update
property records to facilitate reconciliation of the property accounts and
establishment of actual existence and condition of the properties.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
1. Execute a MOA with the barangays setting forth as one of the conditions that
the fund transfer is subject for liquidation pursuant to Section 4(6) of
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001; and
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the fund transfer under Due from
LGUs account instead of Subsidy to LGUs for proper recording, reporting and
presentation in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory Committee to
complete physical inventory of properties; locate and account all determined
unserviceable properties including those found in the schedule of Municipal Accountant
to facilitate preparation of Inventory Report and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property and other supporting documentations for disposal. Thereafter, the Committee
determine the salvage value of the properties based on property ledger cards maintained
by the Accounting Office, appraise the disposal value of all unserviceable properties
through the technical assessment by the Engineering Office, and request for disposal of
the same pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to
realize income from the sale thereof.

Recommendations:
To record local road networks in the books of accounts, we recommended the
Municipal Mayor to direct the:
• GSO and the Municipal Engineer to conduct the physical inventory on the
Local Road Network to come up with the Inventory Report on the Local
Road Network; and
• Municipal Accountant to initially record beginning balance of Local Road
Networks in the books of accounts using as basis the Registry of Public
Infrastructures and the Inventory Report on the Local Road Network.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
• Direct the Municipal Engineer to adhere to the requirements of COA Circular
No. 2013-004 by including in the POW and project design the cost of the
public notification/information at the least possible cost or most economically
effective means; and
• Henceforth, billboard or signboard having vital information shall be installed
at the project site to enhance public awareness and transparency.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
• The Municipal Budget Officer properly monitor the budget allocation for the
PPAs and help the Municipal Development Council (MDC) in the re-
evaluation of unimplemented projects;
• The MDC with the assistance of the MPDO, reassess the necessity of pursuing
the unimplemented projects of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to a
more needed development projects. The Council should also properly plan
and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and
• The Budget Officer and MDC appropriate sufficient funds for the complete
implementation of the identified projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended management to:
• Consider redirecting priorities from regular programs and activities to other
major concerns that need immediate action by the LGU especially the
purchasing of lots for relocation sites of those homeless victims of calamity;
and
• Maximize the utilization of LDRRMF by formulating
programs/projects/activities particularly for disaster preparedness, prevention
and mitigation so that the LGU’s ultimate objective of protecting the lives and
properties of its constituents will be realized.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
1. Create a committee to formulate the Operations Manual for Motorpool
Operations;
2. Create a team to institute and strengthen internal control measures relative to
Motorpool operations;
3. Direct the Motorpool-in-charge to explain the unpaid deliveries of sand and
gravel indicated in the trip tickets attached to the disbursement vouchers for
the payment of fuel expenses.

Recommendation:
We recommended the GAD Council to initiate the establishment of GAD
database as required in RA 9710 and JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management 

(a) require the GSO (i) to maintain PCs and MAO of the PPELC and RPLC that will serve as a tool in the reconciliation of their records to ensure regular monitoring of the PPE accounts; 

(ii) and the MAO to determine the discrepancies in their records with the total PPE procurements for the year; and 

(b) require the GSO to effect the immediate disposal of unserviceable property to prevent further deterioration and loss of commercial value.

We recommended that Management direct the 

(a) Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasury Office to continuously comply with the withholding and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in compliance with existing rules and regulations; and

We recommended that Management

(a) continue to provide an adequate budget and utilize it fully for the full implementation of the GAD plan; 

(b) exert more efforts in implementing other GAD mandates/issues which will benefit other groups of the community; and

(c) continue the collection of data for the GAD database system.
